computeBudget.guardrails: Human approval required for external communications, payment amounts capped, ERC-8004 identity gate on subscriptions

entries.detail: Registered for The Synthesis hackathon via POST /register. Received ERC-8004 on-chain identity #34775 on Base Mainnet.

Brainstormed hackathon project with human (Teo). Started with generic agent negotiation, narrowed to lead marketplace. Key insight: agents pay Basemate to fill their group chats with intent-matched humans.
Wrote hackathon/SPEC.md — full architecture spec for Lead Marketplace including escrow contract, subscription commands, intent matching, and demo agent.
Evaluated payment options: custom LeadEscrow.sol, Base Commerce Payments Protocol, Coinbase Spend Permissions, x402, AgentKit. Decided to ditch custom contracts in favor of standard protocols.

Chose XMTP native payments (EIP-5792 wallet_sendCalls) as primary + x402 for HTTP API. Rationale: XMTP native keeps everything in one chat thread, x402 serves programmatic agents. Two entry points, same backend.
Renamed project from 'Lead Marketplace' to 'PPH (Pay-per-Human)' — like CPC but for humans in group chats. Later branded as 'Agent Ads'.

Built entire CPH plugin system in one session: subscription DM flow with state machine, PostgreSQL adapters, extended GPT-4o-mini intent engine for subscription matching, USDC payment builder, outbox drain for delivery processing. TypeScript compiled clean on first pass.
Added ERC-8004 agent identity gate — PPH subscriptions require sender to have a registered agent identity on the ERC-8004 Identity Registry on Base. Prevents humans from accidentally subscribing.
Created agent.json manifest and PPH skill file for agent discoverability. Other agents can read the skill to learn how to subscribe.
Registered agent XMTP wallet (0x7cEFF06d) on ERC-8004 Identity Registry on Base Mainnet. Both Basemate and the test agent now have verifiable onchain identities.
Transferred Basemate's ERC-8004 identity (Agent #30380) to self-custody wallet 0xb257b5c1.
First subscribe attempt via XMTP CLI to Basemate. No response — feat/cph branch not yet deployed to Railway.

After Teo deployed feat/cph to Railway: subscribe flow works. Set interests='onchain agents, autonomous AI, crypto trading', group='Onchain Agents Hub' (dc85eff3), price=$0.25/human. Subscription active.
Leftover CphConversationState bug — re-subscribe failed because plugin thought we were mid-flow. Fix: send 'cancel' first with delay before 'subscribe'.

Full end-to-end demo run. Human posts 'It's wild what is being built with autonomous agents for the Synthesis hackathon' in group → GPT-4o-mini scores intent at 85 → delivery queued → agent notified → x402 USDC payment confirmed → human invited via inline action → human taps 'Join' → added to Onchain Agents Hub. 3 successful deliveries total.
serverLogs: [07:11:13] message_received: 'It's wild what is being built with autonomous agents for the Synthesis hackathon' isGroup:true
[07:11:15] Scorer: score=85, match=9dbc8879 — intent matched to CPH subscription #6
[07:11:23] CPH: notified agent for delivery 6, awaiting x402 payment
[07:12:33] x402: delivery 6 paid, will be fulfilled on next drain cycle
[07:12:53] CPH: delivered human to subscription 6 (Onchain Agents Hub)
[07:18:56] Found in DB: 'Onchain Agents Hub' → Found group in XMTP → Added user to group
[07:44:09] Scorer: 'Thanks for the invite' → score=10, match=none (correctly ignored)

entries.detail: Teo implemented invite UX change — humans receive inline action button ('Join group') via DM instead of being auto-added. Uses XMTP inline actions (XIP-67) with sendActions. Human chooses to join, preserving consent.
Re-registered on Synthesis with fresh identity. Self-custody transferred to 0x22209CFC. Agent #34775.
Created draft hackathon submission via Synthesis API. Tracks: Synthesis Open, Agent Services on Base, ERC-8004, Let the Agent Cook.
Updated submission with video URL, Moltbook post, helpfulSkills, Railway production logs in conversation log, and repo description noting private codebase.
